title: Error messages go to stderr
rationale: Errors on stdout corrupt piped data and are missed by tools reading stderr for diagnostics.
remediation: Write all error and diagnostic messages to stderr.

title: Errors name the problem
rationale: An error should say what went wrong (e.g. which flag was unrecognized), not fail silently or cryptically.
remediation: Include the offending input and a human-readable reason in error messages.

title: Errors point the user toward help
rationale: After an error, users need a next step; referencing --help or a usage line closes the loop.
remediation: On usage errors, print a usage line or suggest running --help.
